sections.paragraph:
ἀχλύς, ύος, ἡ (Hom. et al.; in prose Hippocr. et al.; Polyb. 34, 11, 15; Ezk 12:7 Aq.; Job 3:5 Sym.; Philo, Cher. 61; Jos., Ant. 9, 56)

① lit. someth. that comes upon one like a fog and obscures vision, mist (w. σκότος as Dio Chrys. 11 [12], 36; Philo, Deus Imm. 130) of darkening of the eyes in a man who is being blinded Ac 13:11 (cp. Il. 16, 344; Od. 20, 357; κατʼ ὀφθαλμῶν δʼ ἔχυτʼ ἀχλύς ‘a mist came over his eyes’ 22, 88; schol. on Apollon. Rhod. 2, 259b; also medic. t.t.: Galen, Medicus 16 [XIV 774 K.]; further exx. in Hobart 44f).

② fig. someth. that beclouds one’s understanding, mistiness, in the eyes of the mind (Heraclit. Sto. 33 p. 48, 14; Plut., Mor. 42c διάνοια ἀχλύος γέμουσα; Himerius, Or. 35 [=Or. 34, 3] p. 146, 20 Colonna ἡ ἀχλὺς τῆς ψυχῆς) 2 Cl 1:6.—DELG.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Hippocr Hippocr = Corpus Hippocraticum, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Sym. Sym. = Symmachus, Greek version of the OT—List 2, beg., 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

someth. someth. = something

Dio Chrys Dio Chrys , I–II a.d.—List 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Il. Il. = Iliad, s. Hom.—List 5

Od Od , s. Hom.; VIII? b.c.—List 5

schol. schol. = scholion, scholia, scholiast

t.t. t.t. = terminus technicus (termini technici), technical term(s)

Galen Galen , II a.d.—List 5

exx. exx. = examples

Hobart Hobart = WH., Medical Language of St. Luke—List 6

Heraclit. Sto. Heraclit. Sto. = Heraclitus the Stoic, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Himerius Himerius , IV a.d.—List 5

2 Cl 2 Cl = 2 Clement—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 160
